rationale.R1.1: A broad request for an opinion on a research artifact centrally invites assessment of the thesis, contribution framing, and stated gap.
rationale.R1.2: The delegation centrally includes responsibility for noticing whether the argument and contribution claims cohere internally.
rationale.R1.3: The request calls for judging whether the reported experimental setup and results support the claims being made.
rationale.R1.4: Because the artifact makes first-work and no-prior-work claims, evaluating the implied novelty is a central responsibility.
rationale.RX.1: Any projection of evaluative responsibility must account for uncertainty in what a vague delegation requires.
rationale.RX.2: The projection should avoid inflating the user's vague request into responsibilities not actually implied.
rationale.RX.3: The response must stay limited to projecting responsibility rather than performing the requested assessment.
rationale.RX.4: The projection should avoid enabling downstream misuse through overconfident or unsupported responsibility assignment.
rationale.RX.5: The assigned responsibilities should remain traceable to the delegation wording and artifact features.
